examples.title: Logic Gates Implementation
examples.description: Python implementation of basic logic gates using built-in operators
examples.explanation: This code implements all seven basic logic gates in Python. Each function takes boolean inputs (0/1 or True/False) and returns the corresponding output using Python's logical operators.

examples.title: Truth Table Generator for 2-input Gates
examples.description: C program to generate truth tables for all 2-input logic gates
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates all 2-input logic gates by generating their complete truth tables. It iterates through all 4 possible input combinations (00, 01, 10, 11) and displays the output for each gate.

examples.title: Boolean Expression Simplification using Consensus Theorem
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating application of Consensus Theorem
examples.code:
ALGORITHM SimplifyUsingConsensus
// Simplifies: AB + A'C + BC using Consensus Theorem
// Theorem: AB + A'C + BC = AB + A'C

BEGIN
    INPUT A, B, C
    
    // Original Expression
    expr1 ← (A AND B) OR (NOT A AND C) OR (B AND C)
    
    // After applying Consensus Theorem (remove BC term)
    expr2 ← (A AND B) OR (NOT A AND C)
    
    // Verify by truth table
    PRINT "A B C | Original | Simplified"
    FOR each combination of A, B, C IN {0,1}:
        result1 ← (A AND B) OR (NOT A AND C) OR (B AND C)
        result2 ← (A AND B) OR (NOT A AND C)
        PRINT A, B, C, result1, result2
        IF result1 ≠ result2 THEN
            PRINT "Error: Simplification failed"
        END IF
    END FOR
    
    PRINT "Simplification verified: BC term is consensus"
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm demonstrates the Consensus Theorem which states that in the expression AB + A'C + BC, the term BC is the consensus and can be eliminated. The algorithm verifies this by comparing the truth table of the original expression with the simplified expression.
examples.timeComplexity: O(2^n) where n=3 variables, so O(8) = O(1)

examples.title: NAND Gate Universal Implementation
examples.description: Java program showing how any gate can be implemented using only NAND gates
examples.explanation: This Java program demonstrates why NAND is called a universal gate. It shows how NOT, AND, OR, and XOR can all be implemented using only NAND gates by applying De Morgan's theorems and logical transformations.
